issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason:
Ran the ENTIRE onboarding end-to-end this cycle (your directive), on fresh un-onboarded accounts, headless: register -> stamp OTP -> verify -> drove all 11 screens in a real browser. Walked intro -> You & Goal (Basics/Body/Goal/Why) -> Training -> Health & Food -> reached fd1e's exact screen: "How should we build your plan?" -> "Coach Nisha builds it now" / "I'll build it myself" -> the "Building your meals..." surface in your screenshot. Captures attached to my notes.

WHAT I FOUND (3 things):
1) The "Building your meals..." wait is the coach-build firing CustomDietBuilder.suggestMealCached x4 meal slots in parallel (Promise.all) — 4 live /ai/suggest-meal LLM calls. Mechanism is unchanged since last cycle, so the ~47s floor I measured (commit 22ba43f6) still holds. 4 LLM calls can't hide behind the short choice screen no matter how warmed — that's the real floor.

2) The coach-build screen is gated by FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS. In CODE it now defaults OFF (you had me remove the "How should we build your plan?" choice for i-20260620-1469), so a from-source build routes straight into the always-custom builders and never shows "Building your meals...". But your release build sets VITE_FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS=true, so users DO see the coach-build path. Confirm that's intended for release (coach-build ON) vs the 1469 always-custom default — they diverge.

3) Found + fixed a rotted QA onboarding driver: the intro carousel went single-tap (i-20260614-fb14) but the capture driver still did a 3-click loop and timed out on a disabled button. Fixed in commit 653a7f9c so automated onboarding capture works again.

FIX (unchanged recommendation): option (b) instant template-first-fill + background LLM refine. diet_builder_service.preview() already returns a real-Meal template plan in ms; fill all 4 slots instantly (<1s perceived), then let the suggest-meal calls swap each slot in when ready. Cost-neutral, reuses the finalize pipeline. It's ready to build in a focused full-stack tick — the ONLY thing I need from you is a yes/no on the brief swap UX (user sees a template meal for a moment, then it upgrades to the AI pick). Veto it and I'll instead just add a real progress UI (slot-by-slot fill) behind the spinner. Either way, say the word.
